programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It

programme.title: Location Location Location
programme.sub-title: East London, Brighton And Hove Revisits
programme.desc: This week, we're catching up with three house hunters, with two very different needs, but one shared goal. In 2018, schoolteacher Katy and her comedian husband Charlie, baby Lola, and pet pooch Harper were on the hunt for their first family home in Brighton. Having moved in with Katy's mum to save for a deposit, they had not only grown used to home comforts, but also the vibrant village atmosphere of pricey Fiveways; an area where the property dreams didn't match their budget. With Phil's help, they widened the net, viewing four fantastic finds in and around Brighton. Two years later, Phil's back to see whether life on the coast has been plain sailing. And in 2019, Kirstie was scouring East London with 'granny on the go' Janet. Having been diagnosed with a degenerative back condition, Janet was desperate to ditch her lift-less, third storey flat in exchange for a ground-floor forever home. The tricky East London property market was proving tough to navigate though, and Kirstie soon found out about Janet's hidden desire for a project. One year on, Phil's eager to see if she got her wish.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location Australia
programme.desc: This week we're in double trouble in one of Australia's toughest markets with two couples who can't afford to take a wrong step. We're on the hunt for houses in some of Melbourne's most popular suburbs with Helen and Tom, a couple of downsizers whose business went bust last year, and also Robbie and Bec, who are trading Melbourne's hip bars for the burbs on a tiny budget. But buying houses can be an emotional business and both couples will find adapting to change is not so easy.

programme.title: Selling Houses Australia
programme.desc: Amanda, Paul and David are siblings who sadly lost their doctor parents in the MH17 plane crash over Ukraine in July 2014. They are selling their five-bedroom family home on a bushy 12 acres near Toowoomba. A touching story as the family is well-loved and respected in the area. Also, they're prepared to donate some of the profits to Medicines Sans Frontiers.

programme.title: Selling Houses Australia
programme.desc: In a program first, bushwhackers Winty, Albone and Blaze attempt to modernise a derelict church from the 1920s. But location and building makes this no easy sale.

programme.title: Love It Or List It Australia
programme.desc: Joel and Anna live in the worst house on the best street and after plans to knock it down fell through, are now living in a space that is not conducive to their lifestyle with two small children.

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Architect Ariane dreams of a home that blurs inside and out and her methods are as unconventional as her materials. Will she convince her husband it has all been worth it, and stay within a $500,000 budget?

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It

programme.title: Phil Spencer: New Zealand's Best Homes
programme.desc: Phil visits a coastal farm with sculptures that has to be seen to be believed and tours an incredible bachelor pad at an exclusive, private golf course.

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Soaring property prices have put inner-city living out of the reach of many. But not for architect Ben and artist wife Tania, who decide to think laterally and get creative buying a tiny 64 square metre discarded scrap of land in one of North Melbourne's grittier back lanes. Their plans - to build a three storey, three bedroom, two bathroom home with a large garden - all for the not-so-grand price of $600,000. But their slim budget is in total contrast to their expansive ideas. Ben is working on a revolutionary concreting method using a giant robot that can transform boring concrete walls into soaring works of art. And while it's an interesting idea in theory, using their own family home to test it is high stakes indeed! With the family crammed into rental accommodation while Ben finesses his master work, the pressure mounts while the months drag on. Will his plans to transform concrete buildings into habitable architecture works of art ever become a reality?

programme.title: Selling Houses Australia
programme.desc: Jason and Christine are budget sellers who hate agents. They can't understand why you would part with thousands of dollars in commission when you could sell the home yourself. After all, it can't be that hard. Seven months ago they listed their modest Brisbane house on a do-it-yourself sell internet site. To save even more money, they didn't bother to order a sign or any marketing materials. The sole advertising push was down to one single website. It's no great surprise that the house hasn't sold and there's been precious little interest. Can Andrew persuade our budget-conscious couple that agents are worth the commission, or will they insist on sticking with the DIY approach?

programme.title: Truck Night In America
programme.desc: This program makes history with the toughest truck obstacle course ever built. America and trucks go together and, since 1925 when the first American factory-produced truck rolled off the line, American owners have been devising ingenious ways to make them faster, stronger, and better. Five truck owners will compete in a series of gruelling challenges that test their craftsmanship and driving ability to the max. Each episode features unique challenges that test trucks in a way that's never been seen before. The challenges culminate in The Green Hell, a three-mile obstacle course which includes challenges that send trucks flying off jumps, over a mountain of crushed cars and even through a snake-infested swamp. Helping them along the way are four experts in the off-road world - they coach, they build, they fix, but the one thing they can't do is drive. In the end, the truck who finishes is crowned the champion - if they can make it that far. In this episode, the Big Dogs are back, along with incredible craftsmanship and massive tires, featuring a 1951 Chevy pick-up and 1971 GMC pick-up. The Silo Pulldown and Four Horsemen show no mercy in vanquishing trucks, and it's a run for the ages in the Green Hell.

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: The buyers get wind of some interesting units in Santa Clarita; Ivy and Isaiah chose quantity over quality; Brandi gets fancy sneaking a unit from everyone else; Kenny finds himself searching for his soul sister.

programme.title: Truck Night In America
programme.desc: This program makes history with the toughest truck obstacle course ever built. America and trucks go together and, since 1925 when the first American factory-produced truck rolled off the line, American owners have been devising ingenious ways to make them faster, stronger, and better. Five truck owners will compete in a series of gruelling challenges that test their craftsmanship and driving ability to the max. Each episode features unique challenges that test trucks in a way that's never been seen before. The challenges culminate in The Green Hell, a three-mile obstacle course which includes challenges that send trucks flying off jumps, over a mountain of crushed cars and even through a snake-infested swamp. Helping them along the way are four experts in the off-road world - they coach, they build, they fix, but the one thing they can't do is drive. In the end, the truck who finishes is crowned the champion - if they can make it that far. In this episode, the Big Dogs are back, along with incredible craftsmanship and massive tires, featuring a 1951 Chevy pick-up and 1971 GMC pick-up. The Silo Pulldown and Four Horsemen show no mercy in vanquishing trucks, and it's a run for the ages in the Green Hell.

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: It's the grand final and one of the five comics will be going home with Greg Davies's massive golden face. Featuring peas, teas, and absent keys.

programme.title: Idiot TV
programme.desc: A series of clips featuring the biggest fools on the planet. It's gold medal buffoonery collected and curated like never before, with rewinds, slow-mo's and close ups.

programme.title: Idiot TV
programme.desc: A series of clips featuring the biggest fools on the planet. It's gold medal buffoonery collected and curated like never before, with rewinds, slow-mo's and close ups.

programme.title: Superstars (WWF)
programme.desc: Featuring the stars of the World Wrestling Federation from wrestling matches and interviews to updates on ongoing feuds.

programme.title: WWE Raw Highlights
programme.desc: Catchup with the latest beat downs and aerial assaults in World Wrestling. Featuring some of the biggest and baddest wrestling superstars straight off the satellite.

programme.title: Aussie Pickers
programme.desc: Join Lucas Callaghan and Adam McDonald, Australia's own pickers, as they scour the country's back roads and barnyards in the quest to turn one man's trash into another man's treasure. Along the way we meet some of Australia's most authentic and colourful characters, whose stories reveal a rich Australian history, and what the pickers find will surprise and astound you.

programme.title: Spectacular Spider-Man
programme.desc: An animated television show that focuses on a sixteen-year-old Peter Parker, and the origins of Spider-Man.

programme.title: Todd McFarlane's Spawn
programme.desc: With a serial killer on the loose, Wanda's search for her husband Terry hits a detour when Wynn's men follow and corner her. Appearing on the scene to eliminate the agents, Spawn allows Wanda to see him for the first time - but she's horrified.

programme.title: Todd McFarlane's Spawn
programme.desc: While Lisa Wu is investigating the murders of two other reporters, Terry is arrested by the police. Wynn blames Merrick for all his recent problems finding Terry; as punishment, he sends another agent to kill Wanda and Cyan.

programme.title: Shipping Wars
programme.desc: Follow independent shippers who have discovered that fortunes can be made by transporting items that traditional carriers won't touch. Each episode of Shipping Wars dives into the cutthroat world of these heavy-duty movers as they battle for the chance to transport the un-shippable. The competition begins with uShip, the world's largest online auction house for independent truckers. From oversized loads and bulky packages to the truly bizarre, these shippers will haul anything if the price is right. From rookie haulers to seasoned veterans, no shipment is too big for these professionals to handle. Marc, Jarrett, Jennifer, Roy and his beloved cat Muffy are back, along with new cast members Chris and Robbie. One of the industry's most unique shippers, Chris's edge in the business comes from his truck, which runs off of a waste vegetable oil system that he invented himself. While he's on the road, his girlfriend and business partner Robbie books loads and maps out routes while taking care of her son, Carter. Don't let their age fool you, this South Carolina couple juggles parenthood, running a successful business, and taking care of their golden retriever, Gix, who is a permanent passenger on the road.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Take a look inside the doors of a family-run pawnshop in Las Vegas, where three generations of men from the Harrison family, grandfather Richard, son Rick and grandson Corey jointly run the business, with clashing and camaraderie every step of the way. This family lives and breathes the pawning business, using their sharp-eyed skills to carefully assess the value of items their colourful customers bring in, objects ranging from the obscure to the truly historic.

programme.title: Big Easy Motors
programme.desc: From a 1957 Chevy Bel Air convertible, to a 1967 Shelby Mustang GT, Charles Handler turns piles of junk into custom cars. He searches all over Louisiana, including the backwoods and the bayou, for classic cars no one wants, invests his own money to fix them up and restore them, and then sells them to the highest bidder. Their cars can sell for upwards of $50,000, so there's a huge profit to be made. But then again, if they put in the work and the car doesn't sell for what they expect it to, they could come home with no profit at all.

This family lives and breathes the pawning business, using their sharp-eyed skills to carefully assess the value of items their colourful customers bring in, objects ranging from the obscure to the truly historic.

programme.title: Road to Riches
programme.desc: There are collectors and then there's Mick. He's filled his large house with Star Wars memorabilia - every square inch of it. The trouble is Mick's super tolerant wife has decided their newborn baby - appropriately named Leia after Princess Leia - needs some room too! Lucas and Mick pick out pieces that will fetch great money - including a full-size Jar Jar Binks model that needs restoring - before putting it all under the hammer at a specialty auction. Lucas hits the country roads to meet farmer George. It's been drought for too long, and farmer George decides he needs to cash up a few old items, and Lucas is the man to get the job done. Some old football caps of George's great grandfather have been sitting around gathering dust, so he decides to let them go - and one of them is a mid 19th century English test cap! Meanwhile, Adrian has invited Lucas in to look at a piece given to him by his father that might be worth a fortune. It's a jacket reputedly worn by Jimi Hendrix!

programme.title: True Blood
programme.desc: Tara resurfaces in Bon Temps. At the Vampire Authority headquarters in New Orleans, Bill and Eric meet Salome and become acquainted with the Authority's interrogation techniques. Pam recalls her human life as the madam of the Comstock Brothel in San Francisco, and her first encounter with Eric. Werewolves JD and Rikki pay tribute to Marcus, but Alcide refuses to participate or to take his rightful place as the new packmaster. Sam and Luna argue after Martha Bozeman shows up wanting to see her granddaughter. Fearing Russell's return, Sookie procures a home-protection system.

programme.title: John From Cincinnati
programme.desc: Eyes around Imperial Beach open to a range of strange circumstances. Butchie and Kai, asleep on the beach, narrowly avoid a roaring beach comber, while back at home Mitch watches Cissy rest peacefully. Cass lays sprawled across her Porsche's interior, and Dickstein wakes up to find Daphne under the covers, doing some very un-Daphne-like things to him. From the creators behind Deadwood and surf-noir writer Kem Nunn, this Showcase Original Presentation is set in Imperial Beach, California, the last great surf-break before Tijuana. This is a story about the Yosts; a family of surfers whose awesome athletic talents have seemed for generations to come with a curse attached. The Yosts' reign and reputation has been eroded by years of bad luck, addiction and hubris. A stranger named John arrives - and the Yosts' banal existence is lifted into something profound, miraculous and, possibly, universal. The distinctive theme song is by the late Joe Strummer and the Mescaleros.

programme.title: Carnivale
programme.desc: Jonesy gives Ben the job of cleaning out the baggage trailer, prompting snickers and stifled laughter from the rousties. Ben finds the trailer sitting dilapidated and forlorn under a gnarled tree and starts going through the dusty contents. Finding an old suitcase, Ben opens it to reveal a carefully wrapped tuxedo and top hat, the same as the man was wearing in a dream. Ben also notices a faded photograph, which he is shocked to see depicts his mother as a young woman on one side and on the back the mysterious letters HS & Flow. Ben pockets the photo and leaves the mystery-filled trailer, only to be accosted by Samson, angry at Ben for not working with the other rousties. When Ben tells of his job at the baggage trailer, Samson reveals that the trailer doesn't exist - it's an old carny gag. When Ben shows Samson the photo to prove he was in the trailer, Samson realises that there's more to the boy than he had thought.

programme.desc: As the carnival shuts down in the terrible moments following Dora Mae's tragedy, a foreboding pall falls over the troupe. Inside the Cootch tent, Stumpy Dreifuss lays next to his daughter, keeping vigil in anguished silence, while his wife screams hysterically searching for their other daughter, Libby. Ben, meanwhile, still wanders in the surreal mine shaft. Spotting men lying on the ground ahead, he ducks under a beam and is rocked by an explosion. When he gets up, he finds himself in a foxhole, surrounded by bodies and wearing a WWI uniform.

programme.title: New Nurses
programme.desc: Susanne realizes she might have to leave the program and Erik ends up treating his own father.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Attempting to woo a gaming company to PiperNet, Richard inadvertently angers one of his prized allies. Dinesh deals with a betrayal. In preparation for a big launch, Gavin leaves his underlings with a cryptic message. Jared gets inside information from Big Head.

programme.title: In Treatment
programme.desc: When Rhonda arrives at Brooke's home distraught over her granddaughter's disappearance, Brooke seizes the opportunity to illuminate how Rhonda's unattainable expectations have marked Laila's entire life.

programme.title: In Treatment
programme.desc: In the wake of her much-needed self-analysis, Brooke contemplates how startling revelations might impact her future with her son and inform her relationship with Adam.

programme.title: Sex and the City
programme.desc: Charlotte is horrified to discover she's dating an uncircumcised man.

programme.title: Sex and the City
programme.desc: Carrie exchanges an "I love you" with Mr Big, but his response isn't exactly what she was hoping for.

programme.title: Sex and the City
programme.desc: Carrie tries unsuccessfully to leave a few things at Mr Big's apartment.

programme.title: Sex and the City
programme.desc: Carrie discovers that Mr Big is going to Paris for a while.

programme.title: Sex and the City
programme.desc: Samantha dates a sports fan whose sex life is in sync with his team's successes.

programme.title: Sex and the City
programme.desc: In an attempt to get over Mr Big, Carrie makes a date with a special friend, and tries to make it a fuller relationship.

programme.title: How To With John Wilson
programme.sub-title: How To Invest In Real Estate
programme.desc: After renting the same apartment for years, John contemplates an unexpected opportunity to become a homeowner.

programme.title: Lockerbie: A Search For Truth
programme.desc: After losing his daughter in the bombing of Pan Am Flight 103, Dr Jim Swire begins a quest for truth, searching for why this was allowed to happen and exactly who was responsible.

programme.title: Lockerbie: A Search For Truth
programme.desc: As Jim continues his campaign for truth and justice, two suspects are indicted for the bombing and his focus has to shift to the fight to bring the men to trial.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A petty officer's dog tags are found in a forest by a group of teens. While searching for the campsite of the sailor, the NCIS team find that there could be a serial killer hiding in the forest.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The NCIS team must stop a teenage student from detonating a bomb attached to his body, while taking the other students hostage at a Quantico base high school. As Gibbs enters the classroom, Tony is left in charge.

programme.desc: A senator's daughter is the victim of a home invasion. The team investigate to determine if it was a random crime or a targeted attack due to her father's crackdown on international cyber warfare.

programme.desc: Captain Sharon Raydor investigates when Sgt Gabriel shoots a murder suspect he's certain is armed, but his account doesn't match the evidence.

programme.desc: The cases of the Naval Criminal Investigative Service's Washington, D.C. Major Case Response Team, led by Special Agent Leroy Jethro Gibbs.

programme.desc: A probation officer is shot and killed, and one of the suspects turns out to be Horatio's biological son.

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: The ability to find justice for John Curtis is put in jeopardy when he is freed on bail, the accuser is killed, and the team uncover a possible conspiracy to frame him.

programme.desc: Pride assembles a team of hacker Elvis Bertrand, retired Green Beret Sydney Halliday, journalist Oliver Crane, and attorney Elliott Newman, to search for a loophole when he faces a grand jury.

programme.desc: A young woman is strangled, and Yee connects the modus operandi to a potential serial offender, leading Riley to revisit a case that went cold on his watch; Price makes a risky move to bait one of their own on the stand.

programme.desc: Gibbs and the team are called in when a couple of kids stumble upon the body of a missing Marine in a frozen lake. The team then has to work to uncover the Marine's connection with a deadly street gang.

programme.desc: When a Pentagon cryptographer is found dead, the NCIS team has to discover whether the person was or wasn't a Pentagon mole, and if the person committed suicide or was murdered.

programme.desc: When a woman is found in Central Park, evidence points to two frequent park-goers: a street vendor and a tech billionaire; in the face of extreme political pressure, McCoy takes drastic action to make the case.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.sub-title: Don't Give A Monkey A Gun
programme.desc: Walden and Alan get divorced, but the latter chooses a selfish parting gift that jeopardises his relationship with Lyndsey.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Recalls the life and presumed death of Charlie Harper as it finally dawns on Alan that he never had proof that his brother died.

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: A Clogged Pore, A Little Spanish And The Future
programme.desc: George finds out that the high school offered his job to Coach Wilkins and quits. Working at Dale's store won't be enough to make ends meet, so George and Mary both hit the newspaper's classifieds, without much luck. To George's surprise, Mary later gets a job working with Brenda Sparks at the bowling alley after revealing she is desperate for one good thing. Georgie wants to make extra money for the baby and comes up with the idea to sell cigarettes in the gaming room. Connie goes with him to Mexico for a cheap supply. Connie later calls George, saying that she and Georgie got arrested at the border. Meanwhile, Sheldon gets his first pimple, alarming him about puberty and pending adulthood. President Hagemeyer and Dr. Linkletter end up missing their youth. Sheldon later has a nightmare that he becomes each of his parents, Connie and Georgie, all adults who seem miserable to him. Missy is able to calm Sheldon, getting him to focus on things he's looking forward to in adulthood and that.

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: Four Hundred Cartons Of Undeclared Cigarettes And A Niblingo
programme.desc: George Sr. bails Meemaw and Georgie out of jail. Also, Sheldon and Missy meet Mandy and Mary feels unwelcome at church, in the sixth season premiere of Young Sheldon.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: As Halloween exposes some differences between Adam and Jackie, Barry and Lainey struggle with their long-distance relationship.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: As Adam makes a commercial to help his dad take on a competing furniture store, Barry and his friends look to Beverly for dating advice.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake and Amy are excited to go on their first real vacation together, but Amy's perfectly planned cruise ship itinerary is thwarted when they discover an old adversary is on board with them.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake brings Amy to meet his mother, but a surprise appearance by his dad throws a wrench in his special day. Meanwhile, Rosa and Charles struggle with a new mandate regarding body cams.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls
programme.desc: Caroline tries to fit in with Max and the diner gang's wild St Patrick's Day celebration, but she longs to practice her former traditions at the Plaza Hotel.

programme.title: 2 Broke Girls

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Kramer is active in the reopening of an old movie theatre. He also is keeping an eye on a friend, Lloyd, who's recovering from a nervous breakdown. Lloyd has a pack of Chinese gum, that Kramer insists everyone tries. George thinks the cashier short-changed him.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Elaine is dating a jazz saxophonist who's sponge-worthy but "he doesn't really like to do everything". Jerry tells one of the members of his band that the saxophonist and Elaine are "hot and heavy". Kramer stocks up on supplies, including 50 cans of Beef-A-Reeno.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: It's Doug's high school reunion, and he's going stag, while Carrie relaxes at home and tries to watch a movie with Arthur.

programme.title: Will & Grace

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.desc: From over 1000 applicants, 72 artists made it to the heats, 8 made it to the semi-final and just 3 to the final, where our worthy winner for this sixth series of Portrait Artist of the Year was selected. In this final programme, the winner embarks on a 10,000 pounds commission to create a portrait of music legend, Nile Rodgers. Charged with painting this formidable character, behind so many iconic tracks that have filled dancefloors across the world since the 1970s, can our artist capture the man behind the music? We see our winning artist build a relationship with the sitter, witness the development of the painting, and hold our breath as the final portrait is unveiled at London's Royal Albert Hall. Stephen Mangan and Joan Bakewell are our presenters.

programme.title: Elon Musk's Twitter Takeover
programme.desc: The story of Elon Musk's long and often troubled relationship with Twitter, charting his journey from one of the site's most provocative users to its sole proprietor.

programme.title: Project Greenlight: A New Generation
programme.desc: A docuseries focusing on the next generation of talented female filmmakers who are given the chance to direct a feature film. Issa Rae will appear in every episode as an Executive Producer, providing guidance and mentorship to these aspiring filmmakers.

programme.title: Faye
programme.desc: Hollywood legend Faye Dunaway candidly discusses the triumphs and challenges of her illustrious career. Through those reflections, she explores personal discoveries, including her struggles with mental health issues.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Portillo discovers how derelict Victorian London is being rejuvenated, puts in a shift at a Cambridgeshire brick factory, and meets the immigrant community built from its bricks.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo sees how Lincolnshire farmers utilised rails to improve their harvests, visits Lincoln's most impressive cathedral, and looks to the future of rail freight.

programme.title: Stars of the Silver Screen
programme.desc: Timothy Francis Robbins was born October 16, 1958 and is an American actor, filmmaker, and musician. He is known for portraying Andy Dufresne in the film The Shawshank Redemption, and has won an Academy Award and two Golden Globe Awards for his roles in the films The Player (1992) and Mystic River (2003).

programme.title: Icons Unearthed: Star Wars
programme.desc: Marcia Lucas, Oscar-winning film editor and ex-wife of George Lucas, sits down for her first-ever on camera interview and provides unique insight into the rise of Lucasfilm.

programme.title: Stars of the Silver Screen
programme.desc: Timothy Francis Robbins was born October 16, 1958 and is an American actor, filmmaker, and musician. He is known for portraying Andy Dufresne in the film The Shawshank Redemption, and has won an Academy Award and two Golden Globe Awards for his roles in the films The Player (1992) and Mystic River (2003).

programme.title: Project Greenlight: A New Generation
programme.desc: A docuseries focusing on the next generation of talented female filmmakers who are given the chance to direct a feature film. Issa Rae will appear in every episode as an Executive Producer, providing guidance and mentorship to these aspiring filmmakers.

programme.title: Europe's Waterways
programme.sub-title: Germany - From Lake Muritz Via Berlin To The Floodplains Of The Spree
programme.desc: Venturing upstream on Europe's waterways, the series discovers five regions of the continent from an unknown perspective and visits people who live from, on and with water. Rivers, canals, lake regions: they have shaped Europe's cultures for centuries - they are a place to live, transport arteries and sources of energy. Five journeys on watercourses broad and narrow lead to Italy, Germany, France, Poland, Holland and Belgium. Along the way: new projects for the future of water in Europe.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: An eye-opening behind-the-scenes look at the making of Depeche Mode's most notable music videos.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: A fascinating look at the secrets and ideas behind the most iconic music videos ever made.

programme.title: Too Young To Die
programme.desc: "If he ever lived to be 30, I'd be surprised", his mother once said in an interview. Sadly, she was right. On April 5, 1994, the 27-year-old rock star killed himself in his house in Seattle.

programme.title: The Canary Islands
programme.sub-title: La Gomera And El Hierro - A Land Before Time
programme.desc: Off the African coast in the middle of the Atlantic, underwater volcanoes raged for millions of years and created a volcanic archipelago: the Canary Islands. Each of them has its own face. This series tells of natural spectacles, unique natural areas and the formation of the volcanic archipelago.

programme.title: World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: The Dovre line runs from Oslo to the remote northern wonderland of Trondheim, passing through the vast Gudbrandsdal valley and across the awe-inspiring Dovrefjell mountain plateau.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: On the west coast of Scotland are a group of islands that are so remote they almost seem to have been forgotten by the rest of the world and are populated by just a few hardy souls. Crossing the Sea of the Hebrides Paul sets off to explore the Shiants; heads east to the romantic Isle of Ewe, and on to the Summer Isles, before travelling to the mighty cliffs of Handa. This tour sees Paul go diving at Scotland's first 'scallop ranch', kayak to the Summer Isles, and visit the eerie but beautiful Island of Gruinard which was once considered to be so solitary and remote, it was chosen to be the testing ground for Britain's biological warfare experiments during World War II.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Among the islands of the Hebrides there's one that can rightly claim to have a separate identity; Islay which was once the capital of the ancient Lordship of the Isles. This programme sees Paul travel from Finlaggan where the famous ruler Somerled once held court, along Islay's windswept coast, through its historic ports and villages to end on its highest mountain. Along the way he will join a dedicated bunch of Ileach who are keeping the seafaring traditions of the island alive, discover the secrets of Islay's illicit whisky distilling past and travel to the stunning Mull of Oa on the island's south Coast, before climbing Ben Vicar for a breath-taking view across the Hebrides.

programme.title: Mystify Michael Hutchence
programme.desc: "Mystify Michael Hutchence" is a powerfully intimate and insightful portrait of the internationally renowned frontman of INXS, Michael Hutchence. Deftly woven from an extraordinary archive of rich imagery, Michael's private home movies and those of his lovers, friends, and family, the film delves beneath the public persona of the charismatic Rock God and transports us through the looking glass to reveal a multifaceted, intensely sensitive and complex man. A violent event strikes Michael and changes his life dramatically, fracturing his sense of self and robbing him of his connection to life. Amidst the darkness, Michaels newborn daughter, Tiger, becomes his one bright light. "Mystify Michael Hutchence" is written and directed by Richard Lowenstein, director of the majority of both INXS and Michael's music videos and the 1986 feature film, "Dogs In Space", in which Michael played the lead.

programme.title: Kylie: Infinite Disco
programme.desc: A one-off promotional live stream concert by Kylie Minogue in support of her 15th studio album, DISCO.

programme.title: Lizzo: Live in Concert
programme.desc: Celebrate the new year with Emmy and three-time GRAMMY-winning superstar Lizzo, her band The Lizzbians and The Little Bigs, The Big Grrrls with special guests Cardi B, SZA and Missy Elliott for a spectacular show filled with lots of love, positivity and incredible music.

programme.title: The Canary Islands
programme.sub-title: La Gomera And El Hierro - A Land Before Time
programme.desc: Off the African coast in the middle of the Atlantic, underwater volcanoes raged for millions of years and created a volcanic archipelago: the Canary Islands. Each of them has its own face. This series tells of natural spectacles, unique natural areas and the formation of the volcanic archipelago.

programme.title: World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: The Dovre line runs from Oslo to the remote northern wonderland of Trondheim, passing through the vast Gudbrandsdal valley and across the awe-inspiring Dovrefjell mountain plateau.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: On the west coast of Scotland are a group of islands that are so remote they almost seem to have been forgotten by the rest of the world and are populated by just a few hardy souls. Crossing the Sea of the Hebrides Paul sets off to explore the Shiants; heads east to the romantic Isle of Ewe, and on to the Summer Isles, before travelling to the mighty cliffs of Handa. This tour sees Paul go diving at Scotland's first 'scallop ranch', kayak to the Summer Isles, and visit the eerie but beautiful Island of Gruinard which was once considered to be so solitary and remote, it was chosen to be the testing ground for Britain's biological warfare experiments during World War II.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Among the islands of the Hebrides there's one that can rightly claim to have a separate identity; Islay which was once the capital of the ancient Lordship of the Isles. Along the way he will join a dedicated bunch of Ileach who are keeping the seafaring traditions of the island alive, discover the secrets of Islay's illicit whisky distilling past and travel to the stunning Mull of Oa on the island's south Coast, before climbing Ben Vicar for a breath-taking view across the Hebrides.

programme.title: The Brief History Of Cricket Downunder
programme.desc: By the early 90s, players excel in one-day cricket, becoming specialists who transform the game and its dynamics.

programme.title: Too Young To Die
programme.desc: "If he ever lived to be 30, I'd be surprised", his mother once said in an interview. Sadly, she was right. On April 5, 1994, the 27-year-old rock star killed himself in his house in Seattle.

programme.title: Branson
programme.desc: The episode tracks the launch of Virgin Atlantic and Branson's efforts to attract media attention to his brand, including an attempt to break the transatlantic speed record in a boat, and his balloon adventure across the Atlantic in 1986.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: Broadcasters Mark Ellen and Robert Elms discuss the impact of the first four Roxy Music albums. The popular British band were influential through the 70s and 80s.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: An in-depth look back at some of Peter Gabriel's most iconic music videos, including his 1986 hit Sledgehammer, which won a record nine MTV Video Music Awards and was a hit amongst his fans.

programme.title: Too Young To Die
programme.desc: Many followed the meteoric rise of this young man who was mercurial with an extreme personality. It only took seven films to make John Belushi unforgettable.

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael revisits his journey through the Himalaya, travelling through Pakistan, India and Nepal, meeting the Dalai Lama along the way.

programme.title: World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: From Tirano in Italy, this route spirals 1km up the Alps, past glaciers and through mountains, ending at the base of the majestic Matterhorn.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: For this grand tour Paul's journey begins at Loch Hourn on the mainland, takes him over the sea to the to Skye, then heads to the tiny island of Soay before ending on Canna. Along the way, Paul visits the house which was the inspiration for Skyfall, home of Ian Fleming author of Britain's most famous spy, James Bond, embarks on a nerve wracking caving expedition under the summit of Blaven, and discovers the remarkable story of shark fishing off Soay.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: For this final grand tour Paul is heading as far north as you can go in Britain, to the islands of Fetlar, Unst and Yell. These islands, which lie on Scotland's northern frontier, are as much Scandinavian as Scottish and were in fact once part of Norway. Along the way he will sing with the Shanty Yell Men who came together to celebrate their Norse Heritage, learn how to 'roo Shetland sheep the traditional way, and get an opportunity to see up close the wild otters who make their home here in Shetland's pristine waters. And finally, Paul will fulfil a long held ambition to visit Out Stack, the rocky outcrop that is famous for being the 'full stop' at the edge of Britain.

programme.title: A Tale Of Two Thieves
programme.desc: An investigator of the 1963 train robbery in England discusses the heist.

programme.title: The Jinx: Life & Deaths Of Robert Durst
programme.sub-title: The State Of Texas vs. Robert Durst
programme.desc: Galveston, Texas becomes a media circus as the press descends on the sleepy seaside town to cover the trial of Robert Durst for the murder of his neighbour, Morris Black. Robert's multimillion-dollar legal team decides to make an unusual plea, he will admit to having dismembered the corpse, but for the murder will plead not guilty by reason of self-defence and accident. The town of Galveston and the entire country become engaged in the drama of the trial as his lawyers take the unusual step of putting Robert himself on the witness stand.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo learns about a Scottish hero, visits a Highland Games, and discovers how an impressive piece of Victorian engineering ended in tragedy.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Portillo learns how Queen Victoria used to hide from her subjects, discovers how factory workers went deaf, and goes out with a bang in Aberdeenshire.

programme.title: Icons Unearthed: Star Wars
programme.sub-title: Attack Of The Clones/Revenge Of The Sith
programme.desc: Marcia Lucas, Oscar-winning film editor and ex-wife of George Lucas, sits down for her first-ever on camera interview and provides unique insight into the rise of Lucasfilm.

programme.title: Music's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: Did the Scorpions end the Cold War? What is Wu-Tang's multi-million-dollar treasure? Kylie Jenner vs Kylie Minogue.

programme.title: Landscape Artist of the Year
programme.sub-title: Royal Ascot Royal Enclosure Car Park
programme.desc: Artists are charged with capturing the glamorous racegoers at the Royal Enclosure Picnic.

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.desc: Watching Portrait Artist of the Year has inspired many a viewer to take up painting and the celebrity sitters often express a desire to have a go themselves. In this year's celebrity special of Portrait Artist of the Year 6 new celebrity artists are invited to take up the challenge. Actors Mark Gatiss, Fenella Woolgar and James Fleet are joined by presenters Anneke Rice and Sophie Morgan, and comedian Jenny Eclair.

programme.title: Gabon: Earth's Last Chance
programme.desc: This enviro-thriller documentary feature tells the extraordinary story of scientist Professor Lee White from Manchester, who ended up becoming the Environment Minister of Gabon in central Africa.

programme.title: Antarctica: A Year On Ice
programme.desc: A visually stunning chronicle of what it is like to live in Antarctica for a full year, including winters isolated from the rest of the world, and enduring months of darkness in the coldest place on Earth.

programme.title: Most Expensive Christmas Cruise Ship
programme.desc: The World's Most Expensive Cruise offers exclusive access and behind-the-scenes insight aboard the most luxurious ship ever built.

programme.title: The Cevennes
programme.desc: Hidden Beauty in the South of France. Imposing plateaus, karst landscapes, deep gorges and vast forests. In the south of France, the Cevennes stretch across the departments of Lozere, Gard and Herault. In 1970, the Cevennes National Park was established, one of the largest national parks in France. In 2011, UNESCO declared the region a World Heritage Site of Humanity. Over the course of almost a year, this documentary goes on a journey of discovery into a unique landscape and its inhabitants, including rare flora and fauna.

programme.desc: The questionable friendship between Prince Andrew and millionaire paedophile Jeffrey Epstein. An investigation into the British connections to this tawdry tale.

programme.sub-title: Harry And Edward: Princes In Exile
programme.desc: A journey through all facets of royal life past and present, in all its grandeur, splendour and controversy.

programme.title: Questioning Darwin
programme.desc: Three months after graduating from Cambridge, Darwin was offered the chance to sail around the world on the HMS Beagle. The five-year journey was a dream for Darwin, who had a great passion for nature. Yet on the trip, Darwin saw his convictions of a divine world order shattered. It turns out that Darwin was genuinely torn between the religious beliefs he espoused as a young man, and the evidence he collected that would challenge the idea of Creationism.

programme.title: Star Hotel Riot
programme.desc: The Star Hotel riot, occasioned by the closing of a popular pub, was one of the largest riots in Australian history. An estimated 4,000 people fought with police on the streets of Newcastle, New South Wales on the night of 19 September 1979.

programme.desc: Problems plagued the launch of The Simpsons, causing it to miss its original debut date and throwing the future of the entire series into question. What happened next was a Christmas miracle that resonates to this day.

programme.title: Icons Unearthed: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The Simpsons writer's room is the stuff of TV legend. It would churn out hilarious scripts like a Ford automobile assembly line, turning what could have been a niche, off-beat miss into a surprise hit. The phenomenal success of the show has almost always been attributed to the irreverent and groundbreaking writing, but while the writers toiled away, tensions rose among the three creators.

programme.title: Icons Unearthed: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Just as critical to the success of the show as the writers, the animators had drama and tension of their own. The funky, punk rock animation house Klasky Csupo, led by Gabor Csupo had secured the contract of a lifetime, but could they hold on to The Simpsons? Through internal strife and natural disasters, the animators gave The Simpsons their signature look.

programme.title: Stars of the Silver Screen
programme.desc: Jeremy Irons was born 19 September 1948 and is an English actor and activist. His first major film role came in the romantic drama The French Lieutenant's Woman (1981), for which he received a BAFTA nomination for Best Actor.

programme.title: History's Greatest Heists
programme.desc: Since the creation of currency, money has made the world go round and people have done anything and everything in their power to get their hands on a lot of it, including formulating some of the most devious and high-stakes heist attempts of all time. Using dramatic recreations, dynamic storytelling and cutting-edge visual effects, alongside first-person witness accounts from the people who were there, the series delves into the intricate schemes and audacity of the criminal masterminds who risked their freedom for a shot at a lifetime of wealth and riches. Brosnan, who is embedded into each heist through state-of-the-art technology, brings each global news headline to life by putting viewers at the heart of the action and breaking down every aspect of the plan including the conniving team, the mark, the execution and finally the aftermath.

programme.desc: Wally and Dave run a newsstand in New York. Wally, loud-mouthed and cocky, is blind, while quiet, polite Dave is deaf. When a man is murdered outside their newsstand, they are the only witnesses: Dave saw someone's legs and Wally smelled her Shalimar perfume. But the police think they've collared their first deaf and blind murderers. Forced to go on the run to clear their names, Wally and Dave somehow manage to outrun and outwit the entire New York City police force and a mysterious band of ruthless criminals, including a beautiful, enigmatic killer and her ice-cold partner.

programme.title: Inside the Factory

programme.title: The Great Australian Bake Off

programme.title: Paul Hollywood Eats Japan
programme.desc: Paul's spending his first week in Japan's mega city capital, Tokyo. He has a lesson in Japanese restaurant etiquette, sees Godzilla and visits one of the worlds cheapest Michelin starred restaurants. He sniffs out an extraordinary bakery which puts a very big smile on his face, then samples the Japanese practice of solo dining and the even weirder practice of solo Karaoke. Finally, Paul heads off on a karting tour of the city with a man called Lady Beard. After fishing for their lunch, they cuddle some puppies, risk life and limb on the streets of Tokyo and finish Paul's first week with a three-course dinner up the posh end of a jumbo jet well, sort of.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Alison Hammond and Noel Fielding host as the bakers take on the challenges of caramel week. In the signature challenge the contestants craft some biscuits before taking on a caramel technical.

programme.title: Masterchef UK: Celebrity
programme.desc: The first challenge is a Knowledge and Skills Test, where the celebrities have the terrifying task of facing judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace one by one.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Ultimate Home Cooking
programme.desc: In this episode Gordon proves it's possible to make sensational food that doesn't cost a fortune. Gordon's mother helps cook wonderfully aromatic spicy braised oxtail followed by chilli braised pears with star anise dust.

programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals
programme.desc: Jamie's rustling up Moroccan bream, couscous salad, pomegranate and harissa, plus British burgers, shred salad, pickles and things.

programme.title: Mary Berry Cooks
programme.desc: Mary Berry is cooking dishes for a family celebration and makes preparing a buffet supper for a special occasion seem almost effortless, with recipes for beef and mushroom stew and chocolate and orange panna cotta.

programme.title: River Cottage Summer Collection
programme.desc: Intrepid chef Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall brings a back-to-nature attitude to modern cooking and consumption. As his mission encompasses real people with real food issues, the host takes viewers into homes around Britain to show ordinary folks how easy and enjoyable it can be to move from pre-processed foods to nutritious natural products.

programme.title: Eat Well For Less
programme.desc: This episode, Gregg and Chris are in Chelmsford, Essex, to help the Thomas family, a couple whose completely different shopping styles are causing arguments in the supermarket aisles.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: We get exclusive access to some of the largest food factories in Britain to reveal the secrets behind food production on an epic scale.

programme.title: Jamie's Sugar Rush
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver investigates sugar's huge contribution to global health problems like obesity and type-2 diabetes, reveals how much sugar is in healthy-looking food, and explores what can be done to help.

programme.title: Mary Berry Cooks
programme.desc: Mary Berry is cooking dishes for a family celebration and makes preparing a buffet supper for a special occasion seem almost effortless, with recipes for beef and mushroom stew and chocolate and orange panna cotta.

programme.title: Eat Well For Less NZ
programme.desc: Tight knit whanau (Family) try to keep to a strict budget but their dream of a trip to Samoa to reconnect with family still feels out of reach. They're also juggling full time study and full-time work so have fallen into the trap of easy, heavily processed, convenience food. Will Chef Michael Van de Elzen and restauranteur Ganesh Raj be able to help them make simple changes leading to better health as well as massive savings?

programme.title: River Cottage Summer Collection
programme.desc: Intrepid chef Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall brings a back-to-nature attitude to modern cooking and consumption. As his mission encompasses real people with real food issues, the host takes viewers into homes around Britain to show ordinary folks how easy and enjoyable it can be to move from pre-processed foods to nutritious natural products.

programme.title: Extreme Cake Makers
programme.desc: Today, fondant meets fashion for an 8th birthday celebration as Karisha attempts to please not just her, but 2 friends as well as they hold a joint birthday party. Going for a catwalk design, with gum paste models of the girls, the big surprise is cake handbags that can actually be picked up and carried. Molly makes a cake take of a favourite farmyard animal when she's asked for a life-like donkey as a surprise 50th birthday cake.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: We get exclusive access to some of the largest food factories in Britain to reveal the secrets behind food production on an epic scale.

programme.title: MasterChef UK
programme.desc: Tonight, the contestants face two more challenges, designed to gauge both their creativity and culinary repertoire. The first is the MarketChallenge, where the seven are tasked with inventing a dish on the spot for judges John and Gregg from a market full of ingredients which include monkfish, quail and lamb.

programme.title: The Great Australian Bake Off
programme.desc: Twelve new bakers are set to undertake 30 intricate baking challenges over the next 10 weeks in order to impress judges Matt Moran and Maggie Beer.

programme.title: Junior Bake Off
programme.desc: The bakers tackle Rav's doll house challenge and recreate their favourite toys in macarons.

programme.title: Jeremy Pang's Asian Kitchen
programme.desc: In the kitchen today, Jeremy is making dishes to feed and please a crowd, starting with delicious Steamed bao with Black Pepper Beef Shortrib filling - a perfect party platter!

programme.title: French Gourmet with Justine Schofield
programme.desc: Justine dives into Dijon's vibrant market, shares her beloved apple crepes, and explores mustard-making at La Moutarderie Fallot.

programme.title: French Gourmet with Justine Schofield
programme.desc: Impossible to traverse Burgundy without indulging in wine, Justine concocts a wine-infused gourmandise. A visit to La Table Maison Lobbies unfolds the secrets of two local delights.

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's My Swedish Kitchen
programme.desc: Rachel heads into the woods in search of mushrooms. Rachel meets an ice-cream maker who inspires her to experiment with unusual flavours as she makes a lemon and dill parfait.

programme.title: Farm to Fork
programme.desc: Courtney and Michael create a must-try recipe at home, their flavourful traybake kung pao cauliflower. Meanwhile, Louis showcases his culinary skills by preparing a mouthwatering crispy skin chicken.

programme.title: Farm to Fork
programme.desc: Courtney dazzles with her culinary skills, presenting BBQ baby peppers with feta yoghurt and coriander salad, as well as a delectable silverbeet and feta pie.

programme.title: Paddock To Plate
programme.desc: Matt Moran travels to South Australia's Eyre Peninsula, a region of both untamed coastline and harsh, arid desert. Local sheep graze on the area's saltbush, becoming a meat flavoured from the inside out. Building on the salt theme, Matt bakes the lamb in a salt crust pastry. South in Port Lincoln, it's time to learn how to catch squid, then on to Coffin Bay for oysters. Thanks to Matt, blue fin tuna is on the menu for lucky racegoers at The Kimba Cup race day.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Alison Hammond and Noel Fielding host as the bakers take on the challenges of caramel week. In the signature challenge the contestants craft some biscuits before taking on a caramel technical.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine With Me
programme.desc: Helen and her outspoken Albanian boyfriend, David, kick off the competition, hoping their Greek menu will bag them the grand.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine With Me
programme.desc: In Essex, three rival duos battle it out to host the best dinner party and win a 1000-pounds prize.

programme.title: Jamie's One Pan Wonders
programme.desc: Jamie cooks two ideal one-pan meals: shakshuka with asparagus and broccoli, and delicious pulled pork that can be batched and frozen for future meals.

programme.title: Tom Kerridge's Sunday Lunch
programme.desc: Tom transforms his favourite Chinese dishes into a delicious Sunday feast. First up, he makes aromatic crispy duck served with pancakes and homemade plum sauce.

programme.title: River Cottage Summer Collection
programme.desc: Intrepid chef Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall brings a back-to-nature attitude to modern cooking and consumption. As his mission encompasses real people with real food issues, the host takes viewers into homes around Britain to show ordinary folks how easy and enjoyable it can be to move from pre-processed foods to nutritious natural products.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: Rick Stein heads north towards the Arctic Circle to explore the culinary delights of Reykjavik. A perfect winter break enriched with a smorgasbord of the best Iceland has to offer.

programme.title: James Martin's Home Comforts
programme.desc: James' travels have provided him with lots of culinary inspiration. He makes a boozy pudding and a fantastic recipe he netted on a recent fishing trip. James is joined by special guest Antonio Carluccio.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands To Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by chef Lisa Goodwin-Allen and they pay a visit to the Isle of Man during the famous TT race warm up week. James serves up proper biker food- pork burgers with a spicy apple sauce, while Lisa visits some local organic juice producers.

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: James Martin starts his American adventure with a visit to one of his favourite places in the USA: the Napa Valley. First, he drops into one of the best food and drink markets in California, before cooking with some delicious local produce at a stunning vineyard. James gets an exclusive look at the kitchen at one of the world's best restaurants, The French Laundry, and tours their stunning kitchen garden with chef and owner Thomas Keller. A visit to the Napa Valley wouldn't be complete without blending your own wine, so James makes his own red to bring back home with him. James takes inspiration from Napa's bountiful larder to cook California-inspired food back in the UK.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Go North
programme.desc: The bikers are exploring Lancashire, the red rose county. From Pendle in the east, to Blackpool in the west, Lancashire has a regional cuisine to match anywhere in the country.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Alison Hammond and Noel Fielding host as the bakers take on the challenges of caramel week. In the signature challenge the contestants craft some biscuits before taking on a caramel technical.

programme.title: How Do They Really Do It
programme.desc: This episode goes inside Subway to reveal how a US sandwich shop became the most ubiquitous fast food brand on the high street, with more outlets across the UK than McDonalds. Discovering the methods Subway uses to create its iconic sandwiches, from speciality bread to full-flavoured sauces, and the business model which has led it to dominate the market, experts and insiders uncover the secrets of its popularity.

programme.title: Jamie's Sugar Rush
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver investigates sugar's huge contribution to global health problems like obesity and type-2 diabetes, reveals how much sugar is in healthy-looking food, and explores what can be done to help.

programme.title: Beautiful Baking With Juliet Sear
programme.desc: These beautiful bakes all involve spices. Juliet serves up a spiced pumpkin pie with a delicious meringue topping, chocolate and chilli cookies, and winter pavlova with spiced apples and pears.

programme.title: Ainsley's Market Menu
programme.desc: It's the last stop on Ainsley's tour and we've arrived in the heart of Adelaide to visit Adelaide Central Market, the largest fresh produce market in the southern hemisphere.

programme.title: Paddock To Plate
programme.desc: Matt Moran travels to South Australia's Eyre Peninsula, a region of both untamed coastline and harsh, arid desert. programme.title: Paul Hollywood Eats Japan
programme.desc: Paul's spending his first week in Japan's mega city capital, Tokyo. He has a lesson in Japanese restaurant etiquette, sees Godzilla and visits one of the worlds cheapest Michelin starred restaurants. He sniffs out an extraordinary bakery which puts a very big smile on his face, then samples the Japanese practice of solo dining and the even weirder practice of solo Karaoke. Finally, Paul heads off on a karting tour of the city with a man called Lady Beard. After fishing for their lunch, they cuddle some puppies, risk life and limb on the streets of Tokyo and finish Paul's first week with a three-course dinner up the posh end of a jumbo jet well, sort of.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Alison Hammond and Noel Fielding host as the bakers take on the challenges of caramel week. In the signature challenge the contestants craft some biscuits before taking on a caramel technical.

programme.title: Great British Bake Off: An Extra Slice
programme.desc: Jo Brand dives into Caramel Week with Prue Leith and superfans Oti Mabuse and Chloe Petts, critiqued by Tom Allen, while showcasing viewer baking photos.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Go North
programme.desc: The bikers are exploring Lancashire, the red rose county. From Pendle in the east, to Blackpool in the west, Lancashire has a regional cuisine to match anywhere in the country.

programme.title: Great British Bake Off Wedding Special
programme.desc: This is the story of the Great British wedding cake - the origins of the most important cake in our lives. The three finalists from last year's "Great British Bake Off" hope to prove that they can master it.

programme.title: Tom Kerridge's Sunday Lunch
programme.desc: Tom transforms his favourite Chinese dishes into a delicious Sunday feast. First up, he makes aromatic crispy duck served with pancakes and homemade plum sauce.

programme.title: Michel Roux's French Country Cooking
programme.desc: Top chef Michel Roux cooks four of his favourite dishes while holidaying in the South of France. He'll be sourcing ingredients from local producers, from a goat farmer in the Cevennes mountains to an organic vegetable grower in the Ardeche. For Michel, cooking on holiday is about keeping things simple. His delicious goats cheese tart and fish with fresh herbs are a feast for the eyes, but will his classic Diplomat Pudding please his hardest critic, his wife Giselle?

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: Rick Stein heads north towards the Arctic Circle to explore the culinary delights of Reykjavik. A perfect winter break enriched with a smorgasbord of the best Iceland has to offer.

programme.title: James Martin's Home Comforts
programme.desc: James' travels have provided him with lots of culinary inspiration. He makes a boozy pudding and a fantastic recipe he netted on a recent fishing trip. James is joined by special guest Antonio Carluccio.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands To Highlands
programme.desc: James is joined by chef Lisa Goodwin-Allen and they pay a visit to the Isle of Man during the famous TT race warm up week. James serves up proper biker food- pork burgers with a spicy apple sauce, while Lisa visits some local organic juice producers.

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: A small, elegant bistro in France has inspired Raymond for this theme: Le Petit Plats. He promises small plates bursting with fresh ingredients and packed with flavour.

programme.title: River Cottage to the Core
programme.desc: With all the incredible fruit that is growing in Britain today, Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall wants to open our eyes to the endless and mouth-watering possibilities at our fingertips. Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall wants to rip up the cookery rule book to show just how deliciously fruit can enhance and transform meat, fish and pudding dishes, proving it shouldn't be restricted to crumbles, chutneys and pies. We'll follow Hugh as he offers his unique take on everything, from savoury strawberries to fruity pizzas. He'll point the finger at the supermarkets' quest for cosmetic perfection and shows us how we can grow our own fruit at home that will always taste fantastic, regardless of how it looks. Hugh kicks off the series with the much-anticipated first fruits of the season. To prove that strawberries can be served as a savoury, he's staging a surprising cooking demonstration with strawberry steak and salad recipes, but will his guests buy into it? He also throws a big party to celebrate the reopening of his barn, a year-and-a-half after it was completely destroyed by fire. He also takes to the sea, the hedgerow and the kitchen garden in search of inspiration for the menu. There'll be crackling-covered, fruit-stuffed loins of roast pork, a delicious lemon tart, and aromatic mojitos using unlikely foraged ingredients, including blackberry leaves and lemon balm.

programme.title: Food Unwrapped Specials
programme.desc: The Food CIA team explore health-boosting science: vegan bacon, sleep-friendly lettuce, sun-protecting foods, sweet potatoes, fibre, and the secret to staying youthful with black garlic.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace receives a load of corn fresh off the boat from Argentina and follows its journey through the largest breakfast cereal factory in Europe as it is cooked, milled and flavoured to become Crunchy Nut Cornflakes.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Alison Hammond and Noel Fielding host as the bakers take on the challenges of caramel week. In the signature challenge the contestants craft some biscuits before taking on a caramel technical.

programme.title: The Great Kiwi Bake Off
programme.desc: This season brings a new group of 10 baking hopefuls to the tent to impress the returning judges Sue Fleischl and Dean Brettschneider.

programme.title: Eat Well For Less NZ
programme.desc: This couple love their brands, their labels and eating out regularly. Money hasn't always been an issue, but Covid forced a change in their circumstances and they're desperate to save money, reduce their waste, cut back on salt, repurpose their leftovers and learn to use their oven! Chef Michael Van de Elzen and restauranteur Ganesh Raj have their work cut out for them this week, but the couple want to change, they just need some help to move them away from the snack isle and into their own beautiful kitchen.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Guest Judge Joe Sugg helps decide the winner from the two remaining chefs. There are dishes celebrating Paddington, James and the Giant Peach, and the Clangers.

programme.title: Mary Berry Cooks
programme.desc: Mary makes a Mediterranean platter of hummus, bread sticks, roasted vegetables and a tomato and mozzarella salad, perfect food for sharing on a summer's evening. For hungry guests, Mary offers lamb dhansak and cottage pie.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Ultimate Home Cooking
programme.desc: Gordon's Ultimate Home Cooking course continues with a flavour-packed Middle Eastern breakfast, lunch and dinner. Recipes include a delicious twist on classic hummous made with beetroot and wonderful lamb koftas full of herbs and spices.

programme.desc: Paul Hollywood, Prue Leith, Matt Lucas and Noel Fielding see in the New Year with 2018 Bake Off runner-up Kim-Joy and fellow baker Jon, and Hermine and Rowan from the 2020 Bake Off bubble.

programme.desc: Tonight, they face just one intense challenge. They must cook two courses that will excite not just John and Gregg, but also some of MasterChefs most inspiring winners, finalists and contestants, who have all gone on to careers in the food industry.

programme.desc: Molly digs deep for her latest commission as her Gran needs a cake to celebrate older Older Peoples' Day. Her stunning 2 ft square allotment themed cake comes complete with a cake and biscuit tool shed, florist paste picket fence, fondant crazy paving, and dozens of sugar paste details including fruit and veg, and even a pair of edible muddy boots. Former fashion designer turned cake artist Nastassja, must make a birthday cake as glamorous as the birthday girl herself, to take centre stage at a dinner party. Made from amaretto and red velvet cake covered in buttercream and gold leaf, her toughest challenge will be adding over 200 wafer paper discs and getting this 2 and a half foot tall cake to hang upside down.

programme.title: Eat Well For Less NZ
programme.desc: A Waikato sharemilker and his city working wife are time poor and rely on quick, bland kitchen short-cuts for the entire family. Dairy farming is a passion and Alan truly cares for, and feeds, his cows well - now it's time for his family to take the same care with their own diet.They want to get to back to basics, enjoy the produce from the Waikato region and cook from scratch. Will Chef Michael Van de Elzen and restauranteur Ganesh Raj's intervention help reinvent mealtime?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: One of the four must leave the competition and this will be decided by the Veteran Judge of this week's showdown - a previous winner who remains a surprise to the chefs.

programme.title: Mary Berry Cooks
programme.desc: For Mary's outdoor party, the dishes are best served cold: salmon fillets with herb sauce and fillet of beef with garlic and mustard cream. And what better way to finish off a summer lunch than Mary's summer pudding.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Ultimate Home Cooking
programme.desc: Gordon proves that home-cooked healthy food can be as good for you as it is delicious. Gordon's daughter Holly helps him cook a wonderful dinner of aromatic lamb chops followed by healthy mango sundae.

programme.title: Great British Bake Off: Festive Specials
programme.desc: New Year's Special with past contestants Chigs, Lottie, Manon and Antony.

programme.title: MasterChef UK
programme.desc: John and Gregg don't witness them cook in the MasterChef kitchen. Instead, they taste each dish in the new Tasting Room. Just like diners in a restaurant, they judge each contestant's food solely on the plate that is put in front of them. After tasting all nine plates, John and Gregg pick their three favourite dishes, giving those cooks a well-earned MasterChef apron and immunity from cooking in the next round.

programme.title: Inside the Factory

programme.title: Jamie's One Pan Wonders
programme.desc: Jamie makes three one-pan delights: speedy smoked salmon pasta, a loaded beef burger with peppers and pickles, and soft sticky toffee apple buns.

programme.title: Great British Bake Off Wedding Special
programme.desc: This is the story of the Great British wedding cake - the origins of the most important cake in our lives. The three finalists from last year's "Great British Bake Off" hope to prove that they can master it.

programme.title: Masterchef UK: Celebrity
programme.desc: The first challenge for the five hopefuls is the brand-new Under The Cloche. Celebrities must create one dish showcasing that ingredient to demonstrate to the judges they have potential.

programme.desc: Parents David & Nadya from Bristol are at their wits end. Like many people in the UK their sons have food allergies and trying to cater to them is making meal times stressful, adding a hefty amount to the weekly shopping bill AND creating a lot of food waste.

programme.title: Paul Hollywood: A Baker's Life
programme.desc: Paul bakes a pistachio and rose petal roulade and shares his ultimate pizza recipe. Back in the tent with Prue Leith, Paul bakes a madeira celebration cake decorated by Noel and Sandi.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Bakers compete in pastry challenges: frangipane tart Signature, flaky Technical, and choux Paris-Brest Showstopper requiring freestanding podium.

programme.desc: Jo Brand hosts Alexander Armstrong, Natalie Cassidy, and AJ Odudu for Pastry Week discussions. Tom Allen reviews audience bakes and Jo highlights viewer photos.

programme.desc: On a mission to taste the world's best breakfast, first the boys are up to their old tricks, when Fred is nearly floored by a 38-litre jar of tequila filled with snakes.

programme.title: Extreme Cake Makers
programme.desc: In London, cake maker Nastassja's pushing the boat out for her latest commission to celebrate little Noah's first birthday. Asked for a Noah's Ark themed cake, Nastassja's planning to position her revolving edible boat, filled with keep sake toy animals on a giant 2 and a half foot tall spurt of water made from 12 layers of vanilla sponge, topped with rice cereal and marshmallow waves. Molly's hoping to create a bit of flutter, as she creates a giant Butterfuly Cake for a birthday surprise. Sculpted from vanilla sponge sandwiched with white chocolate and raspberry buttercream, she'll use all her sugar craft and air brushing skills to capture the insect's delicate and distinctive blue and pink markings in vibrant sugar paste.

programme.desc: It's Food Unwrapped's Healthy New Year. This special episode is stuffed with virtuous food hacks and little-known science secrets to help turbo-charge your health and well-being. Jimmy discovers if chia seeds could be the key to curbing overeating. Briony asks why producers of gluten free products are seemingly charging more for less. Matt lets the dogs out and discover whether raw pet food could be the key to a healthy pooch. And Kate discovers some surprising science about the watermelon's superpowers.

Sculpted to look like it's bursting out of the water, Ben struggles with its complex shape. Baking meets building for Lancashire based Molly, as she sculpts a cake replica of Queen Anne's Summer House to celebrate a construction company's 40th anniversary. Made from red velvet and Victoria sponge, Molly layers the cake like bricks as a special surprise when the cake is cut.

programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals

programme.title: Mary Berry's Love To Cook
programme.desc: Mary Berry loves to explore new ideas that inspire her to make stand-out dishes that really tantalise the tastebuds.

programme.title: Cook Clever, Waste Less with Prue & Rupy
programme.desc: Vikkie and Wayne in London have a bad habit of cooking a nightly back-up meal for their fussy kids. It's making their shopping bills double the national average and causing their food waste to sky-rocket.

programme.title: Paul Hollywood: A Baker's Life
programme.desc: Paul looks back at his early career, from an apprentice baker to working at a five-star stately home. On the menu: fresh fruit doughnuts, pork pie and Danish pastries.

programme.title: Eat Well For Less
programme.desc: Chris and Gregg meet the Hoyland family. Dad Derek, who is coeliac, hasn't eaten the same meal as his family for nearly six years. Mum, Clair, is brand-obsessed, filling the trolley with frozen convenience food.

programme.title: Paul Hollywood Eats Japan
programme.desc: Paul is now heading out to travel more widely across Japan by road, rail and air. On the road, he happens across a world record breaking bakery. On the railway, he lives out his ultimate train spotter fantasy rides the Shinkansen or bullet train as we know it and experiences the best railway buffet food on earth. And a short flight takes him to the sub tropical islands of Okinawa, once celebrated as the healthiest place in the world, but now becoming addicted to American style fast food. Paul also visits two of Japan's best known cities. First he visits Japan's ancient capital Kyoto where, as well as trying his hand at being a Ninja, meat lover Paul attempts to enjoy tofu in its many forms. Then he moves on to Hiroshima. Whilst most of the world only knows this city as the place they dropped the first atomic bomb, these days the city has a growing reputation across Japan for its food. Paul visits possibly the smallest restaurant in Hiroshima to meet its owner, a bomb survivor who continues to cook and serve the city's signature dish. In what Paul fondly remembers as one of his happiest and favourite days in Japan, she teaches Paul to cook the famous Hiroshima-style Okonimayaki. Finally, Paul travels way up north into the snow to take part in a particularly brutal rice festival. It's a contest where the north and south of the town fight it out with 6 metre long bamboo sticks to see whether they'll get a good rice harvest. Paul gets stuck into to both the fighting and the sake!

programme.desc: Uncle Junior is released to house arrest and uses his doctor's office to conduct business. Tony and a labour leader scheme to force jobs and money from a construction site.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: A luckless gambler keeps getting deeper in debt to Richie and Tony. Janice encourages Richie's aspirations to take over the organisation, Dr Melfi infuriates Tony by throwing his insults back at him.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Christopher is seduced by Hollywood's allure and a film executive named Amy. Having told a forbidden tale of Mafia life to his new Hollywood circle, Christopher worries after the plot ends up in a filmmaker's script.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Christopher swears off drugs and proposes to Adriana, but is ambushed by disgruntled employees Matt and Sean. Carmela terrorises a neighbour's sister into giving Meadow a recommendation to college.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: In the ICU after being ambushed by Sean and Matt, Christopher survives a cardiac arrest and says he saw his father in Hell. Paulie worries over a message Chris brings back from the dead.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: A witness to the Bevilaqua murder is unaware that he's identified a mobster as the killer. Tony stalls the FBI and makes preparations to flee.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Dr Melfi's drinking continues before each session with Tony, who is bored and stressed trying to keep a low profile at a sanitation company; a lonely Junior finds companionship in an old friend.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.sub-title: The Knight In White Satin Armour
programme.desc: Tony's pulled back into Irina's life when she attempts suicide. Richie proposes to Janice, but their engagement is short lived.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Tony and his family are under 24-hour surveillance as the FBI attempts to infiltrate the gangster's home. Tony must assuage the twin brother of the man Tony ordered to be whacked.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Racial tensions flare, and Tony suffers another panic attack when he meets Meadow's new boyfriend from college. The FBI finds a new member of the Soprano crew to use as a rat.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Tony recalls his first anxiety attack as a child. Christopher finally becomes a made man but struggles with the pressure of his new position.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Svetlana enlists Russian thugs in her conflict with Janice. Dr Melfi is brutally raped, but her attacker is freed on a technicality, leaving her faced with a crisis of conscience.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Bobby's cancer-ridden father comes out of retirement to do a hit, and Junior is given some unpleasant news about his medical condition.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: A young Bada Bing dancer named Tracee is pregnant by Ralph Cifaretto. When the two argue outside the club, Ralph brutally beats her to death. When Tony sees her lifeless body, he attacks Ralph.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: While having surgery, Uncle Junior dreams that the FBI offered him a cure for his illness if he will testify against Tony. Dr Melfi refers Carmela to a therapist who advises her to leave Tony.

programme.title: The Sopranos
programme.desc: Tony's relationship with Ralph Cifaretto remains strained. However, when Gigi dies suddenly, Tony appoints Ralph as the new captain of Gigi's crew, thereby restoring peace to the Family.

programme.sub-title: To Save Us All From Satan's Power
